opening_complaint.verbatim_patient_text: Everyone is mumbling and ignoring me on purpose, and my ears are ringing constantly! (Spouse, who made the call: 'He can't hear a word anyone says, the telly is on full blast, and tonight he's got it into his head that we're all whispering behind his back. We're at our wits' end.')
opening_complaint.stated_reason_for_presenting_today: Things came to a head tonight — a huge argument at home because he can't follow what anyone says, and he's now convinced the family is whispering to shut him out.

symptom_narrative.onset: spouse says it has crept up very gradually over about five years, and has 'boiled over' in the last little while
symptom_narrative.duration: years, slowly worsening; the upset tonight is acute
symptom_narrative.character: can hear that people are talking but can't make out the actual words, especially higher voices; misses what the grandchildren say; constant ringing in both ears
symptom_narrative.severity_patient_description: he says it is driving him mad and making him feel cut off from everyone
symptom_narrative.location_as_patient_describes: both ears, about the same on each side
symptom_narrative.timing_pattern: there all the time; far worse in a noisy room or when more than one person is talking
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_better: nothing much; a bit easier one-to-one in a quiet room facing the person
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_worse: crowds, background noise, people talking from another room or with their back turned
symptom_narrative.associated_symptoms_volunteered: constant high-pitched ringing in both ears; turning the TV up very loud; feeling left out, withdrawn and lately suspicious that people are excluding him on purpose
symptom_narrative.previous_episodes: no sudden episodes — the spouse is clear it has been a slow slide, never a sudden drop
symptom_narrative.functional_impact: still fully independent with everything physical, but socially withdrawn; growing marital conflict over the TV volume and repeated conversations

past_medical_history.condition_as_patient_states: longstanding gradual hearing trouble that has never been formally assessed
past_medical_history.how_managed: just turns things up louder and asks people to repeat

social_history_volunteered.relevant_life_context: retired, lives with spouse who is present and made the call; increasingly isolated because conversation has become so difficult

communication_style.typical_message_style: loud, defensive, frequently mishears and misinterprets questions; spouse relays much of the history

objective_data_offered.value: spouse: 'He has the TV up at maximum, can't hear us from another room, and asks us to repeat everything. It's been getting worse for years but never a sudden change in one ear.'
objective_data_offered.timing: over the last ~5 years, worse recently

objective_data_offered.value: constant high-pitched ringing in both ears
patient speaking very loudly, appears agitated and defensive, repeatedly mishears the questions asked
spouse confirms no ear pain, no discharge, no recent sudden loss in just one ear, and no room-spinning dizziness
